bullets.text: Full-year CR 93.4 despite Hurricanes Harvey and Irma (first two Cat 4 Atlantic landfalls in the same year); Commercial Lines CR delivered strong profitability exceeding $3B NPW; auto programs estimated ~10 points more profitable than industry average.

NPW grew 16%, adding $3.8B — larger than the 11th largest private passenger auto insurer; Personal Lines new auto applications up 18%; fifth consecutive year of Direct auto new quote and new application growth; Robinson PIF growth just over 30%.

Personal auto PIFs grew 13%; Robinson (auto+home bundle) PIFs grew >30% YOY creating 45% more bundles than in 2016; Progressive Advantage Agency (direct in-house) Robinson PIFs up ~80% YOY; trailing 12-month auto PLE up ~7%.

Investment portfolio total return 5.2% (equity +21%, fixed income +3%); issued $850M of 30-year debt to pre-fund $564M fixed-to-floating securities redemption; Gainshare score 1.79 (best since 2004, 6th consecutive YOY increase); dividend of $1.1247/share.

Favorable frequency trends wider than pricing assumptions; customer mix shifting toward lower-loss risk; Snapshot data showing longer trips by enrolled customers; Property CR 105.1 with severe thunderstorm losses 25.6 points (60% above 20-year average); Harvey+Irma totaled ~6.8 property CR points.

HomeQuote Explorer launched driving online home/condo new applications up 49%; HQX sales yield improved 50%+ since launch; Snapshot mobile app rolled out to 42 states with 33M trips and 300M miles collected; Flo chatbot launched on Facebook Messenger.

Snapshot mobile app (launched late 2016) completed rollout to 42 states; first top-10 U.S. insurer to enable auto quote via chatbot (Facebook Messenger); investments in AI, machine learning, advanced analytics, and digital customer service delivery ongoing.

Flo/Superstore celebrated 10 years with new characters; Parentamorphosis homeowner campaign second round; four concurrent campaigns all performing across both home and auto quote metrics; Direct auto 16% new application growth — fifth consecutive growth year.

Hired ~6,000 external employees in 2017 (6,600+ in 2016); purpose statement 'True to our name. Progressive.' established via employee vote; 25th Gainshare anniversary with 1.79x payout (~$8,100 median employee payout); Keys to Progress donated 500th vehicle since 2013.

Personal Lines expense ratio consistently below 20 points; non-acquisition expenses reduced 0.5+ points over 3 years; Service Center model transitioning to Network Shop operations to improve customer experience, employee work environment, and efficiency.
